business_goals: Optimize road traffic and provide tools for efficient traffic control
Enable fast response to road accidents or threats using real-time analysis
Provide efficient route planning and optimization based on dynamic traffic data
Integrate existing and new sensor infrastructure seamlessly
technical_constraints: Need for interoperability with diverse subsystems
architectural_drivers: Modifiability (Integration of new sensors and event types)

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: Generic architectural views (ArchiMate) were available but lacked constructs to describe scenario influence on components or capture specific design decisions. Some specifications were missing or decisions had not yet been made.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: What is the platform for this component (e.g., PostgreSQL, Linux, IIS)?
Is the component logic fixed or customizable?
What is the query granularity (bulk, low, medium, high)?
What communication type is used (synchronous, asynchronous)?

hardware_view_description: Data originates from a network of fixed sensors (video detectors, acoustic sensors, inductive loops)
Data includes on-board GPS receivers installed in vehicles
End-user interaction via network or mobile interfaces
runtime_constraints: Real-time performance requirements for traffic analysis
High volume of data feeds from sensors
Interoperability with various sensor types and client modules

questions: How are transactions distributed across the distinct repositories (Static, Traffic, Event)?
How are ontology definitions synchronized with database dictionaries?
Is the query granularity optimized for bulk updates vs. individual reads?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: New sensor types and sensor processing software will be integrated in <6 days>
Dynamic Map will be capable of accepting feeds from up to 1000 sensors occurring every <60> sec
Dynamic Map will be capable of processing up to <200> read queries per second
New map features can be introduced and made discoverable with built-in functionality
Updating of data defining the road network can cause a loss of at most 10 values of a single monitoring parameter
Sensor failures will be detected in a configurable period ranging from 3 to 20 minutes
New event types and corresponding customizable detection routines can be introduced in 1 day
System will be able to accommodate and interpret location data from 5000 GPS tracker units updated every 5 sec
A Dynamic Map component can be ported to another platform within 20 days

architectural_approaches.description: Utilizes centralized, specialized repositories for static, traffic, and event data to manage high-volume sensor updates and varied query requirements.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Optimized for high-throughput write operations from sensors and read-heavy query loads for route planning.
Decouples data storage from processing, allowing new sensor types or analysis modules to be integrated via standard repository interfaces.

architectural_approaches.description: Organizes the system into distinct layers (Presentation, Application Logic, Data Access) to manage platform heterogeneity and separate concerns.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Isolates platform-specific dependencies in the presentation and hardware interface layers, facilitating deployment across heterogeneous client devices.
Changes to business logic or UI components can be made independently without affecting the underlying data management or sensor integration.

architectural_approaches.description: Asynchronous communication pattern where sensors and analysis modules publish events to a middleware that routes them to interested subscribers.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Enables real-time response to traffic incidents by pushing updates immediately to relevant modules without polling overhead.
Increases system resilience by decoupling producers (sensors) from consumers (analysis tools), preventing a failure in one from blocking the entire pipeline.

children.scenario_description: System must process 10,000 sensor updates per second.
Real-time traffic incident alerts must be delivered to subscribers in < 2 seconds.
Integration of a new sensor data type must be completed within 10 person-days.
The application logic must be deployable across Android, iOS, and Web platforms with >80% code reuse.
The system must maintain 99.9% availability and data integrity during intermittent sensor network outages.

scenarios.scenario_text: System must process 10,000 sensor updates per second to maintain real-time traffic state.
Real-time traffic incident alerts must be delivered to subscribers in < 2 seconds from the moment of detection.
Integration of a new sensor data type (e.g., environmental sensors) must be completed within 10 person-days.
The application logic must be deployable across Android, iOS, and Web platforms with >80% code reuse to minimize development overhead.
The system must maintain 99.9% availability and data integrity even during intermittent sensor network outages.

scenario_reference.text: System must process 10,000 sensor updates per second to maintain real-time traffic state.

analysis_records.response_measure: Processing latency remains stable; no data loss
architectural_decisions: Data-Centric / Repository Style for state management
Asynchronous ingestion via Event-Driven / Pub-Sub middleware
analysis_records.reasoning: The Pub-Sub middleware decouples the high-frequency sensor writes from the processing logic, preventing backpressure. However, the centralized repository must be specifically tuned for high-write IOPS to avoid becoming a bottleneck.

risks.rationale: A single repository may not sustain 10,000 writes/sec without significant horizontal scaling or specialized hardware, potentially leading to increased ingestion latency.

non_risks.rationale: Modern Pub-Sub systems (e.g., Kafka) are designed to handle this volume of messages natively with minimal overhead.

scenario_reference.text: Real-time traffic incident alerts must be delivered to subscribers in < 2 seconds from the moment of detection.

analysis_records.stimulus: Traffic incident identified by analysis module
analysis_records.reasoning: Using a push-based event model ensures that once an incident is detected, the alert is immediately broadcast to subscribers without the latency associated with polling the repository.

risks.rationale: High volume of sensor data (Scenario P1) could saturate the network, delaying the delivery of high-priority incident alerts.

scenario_reference.text: The system must maintain 99.9% availability and data integrity even during intermittent sensor network outages.

analysis_records.response_measure: 99.9% availability; zero data loss upon reconnection
architectural_decisions: Layered Architecture to isolate network interface logic
analysis_records.reasoning: Persistent queues in the middleware allow the system to store incoming data during downstream outages, while the layered approach allows for specific retry logic in the hardware interface layer.

risks.rationale: Extended outages during high-volume periods (10k updates/sec) could exceed the storage capacity of the message broker's persistent queues.

non_risks.rationale: The Pub-Sub pattern ensures that a failure in the analysis layer does not cause the ingestion layer to lose sensor data.
